author:	sourcery-ai
association:	none
edited:	true
status:	none

<!-- Generated by sourcery-ai[bot]: start review_guide -->

## Reviewer's Guide

Introduces strategy factory interfaces, a compact staging buffer, and dispatch command factories to decouple fleet order creation, pricing, and dispatching while minimizing allocations for pooled dispatch processing.

#### Class diagram for new strategy factories and dispatch data structures

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class V12_002 {
    }

    class IFleetAccountFilter {
        +bool ShouldSkip(Account account, AccountRankInfo rankInfo, HashSet_string activeAccountSnapshot)
    }

    class IFollowerPricingEngine {
        +void ComputeFollowerPrices(OrderAction action, double entryPrice, double stopPrice_out, double t1_out, double t2_out, double t3_out, double t4_out, double t5_out)
    }

    class IFollowerFSMBuilder {
        +FollowerBracketFSM BuildPendingSubmit(Account account, string fleetEntryName, int followerQty, Order entry, Order stop, string ocoId, StagedTargetBuffer stagedTargets)
    }

    class DispatchOrderEnvelope {
        +FleetDispatchSlot Slot
        +int PoolSlotIndex
        +DispatchOrderEnvelope(int poolSlotIndex, FleetDispatchSlot slot)
    }

    class StagedTargetBuffer {
        +int Count
        +StagedTarget T1
        +StagedTarget T2
        +StagedTarget T3
        +StagedTarget T4
        +StagedTarget T5
        +void Add(StagedTarget target)
        +StagedTarget At(int index)
    }

    class MarketOrderCommandFactory {
        +DispatchOrderEnvelope Create(OrderAction action, int followerQty, double entryPrice, double stopPrice, int dispatchTargetCount, int reservedDelta, int poolSlotIndex, long signalTicks, ulong shadowSalt)
    }

    class LimitOrderCommandFactory {
        +DispatchOrderEnvelope Create(OrderAction action, int followerQty, double limitPrice, int reservedDelta, int poolSlotIndex, long signalTicks, ulong shadowSalt)
    }

    class FleetDispatchSlot {
        +double EntryPrice
        +double StopPrice
        +long SignalTicks
        +int PoolSlotIndex
        +int OrderCount
        +int Quantity
        +int TargetCount
        +int Action
        +int ReservedDelta
        +ulong Shadow
    }

    class StagedTarget {
    }

    class Account {
    }

    class AccountRankInfo {
    }

    class Order {
    }

    class OrderAction {
    }

    class FollowerBracketFSM {
    }

    class HashSet_string {
    }

    V12_002 ..|> IFleetAccountFilter
    V12_002 ..|> IFollowerPricingEngine
    V12_002 ..|> IFollowerFSMBuilder

    DispatchOrderEnvelope *-- FleetDispatchSlot

    MarketOrderCommandFactory ..> DispatchOrderEnvelope
    MarketOrderCommandFactory ..> FleetDispatchSlot

    LimitOrderCommandFactory ..> DispatchOrderEnvelope
    LimitOrderCommandFactory ..> FleetDispatchSlot

    IFleetAccountFilter ..> Account
    IFleetAccountFilter ..> AccountRankInfo
    IFleetAccountFilter ..> HashSet_string

    IFollowerPricingEngine ..> OrderAction

    IFollowerFSMBuilder ..> Account
    IFollowerFSMBuilder ..> Order
    IFollowerFSMBuilder ..> FollowerBracketFSM
    IFollowerFSMBuilder ..> StagedTargetBuffer

    StagedTargetBuffer *-- StagedTarget

    FleetDispatchSlot ..> OrderAction
```

### File-Level Changes

| Change | Details | Files |
| ------ | ------- | ----- |
| Define internal strategy contracts for account filtering, follower pricing, and FSM construction to decouple follower logic. | <ul><li>Add IFleetAccountFilter with ShouldSkip to gate accounts based on rank and active snapshot state.</li><li>Add IFollowerPricingEngine with ComputeFollowerPrices to centralize follower stop/target price calculations.</li><li>Add IFollowerFSMBuilder with BuildPendingSubmit to encapsulate construction of follower FSMs from staged targets and order state.</li></ul> | `src/V12_002.SIMA.StrategyFactories.cs` |
| Introduce lightweight data structures to support pooled dispatch processing without heap allocations. | <ul><li>Add DispatchOrderEnvelope struct to pair a FleetDispatchSlot with its pool slot index for dispatch queues.</li><li>Add StagedTargetBuffer struct as a fixed-capacity (5) staging buffer with Add and At accessors, tracking Count and storing StagedTarget entries in fields instead of arrays.</li></ul> | `src/V12_002.SIMA.StrategyFactories.cs` |
| Add market and limit order command factories that build FleetDispatchSlot instances and compute dispatch shadows. | <ul><li>Implement MarketOrderCommandFactory.Create to populate FleetDispatchSlot for bracket-style orders, computing OrderCount, TargetCount, and Shadow via ComputeFleetDispatchShadow.</li><li>Implement LimitOrderCommandFactory.Create to populate FleetDispatchSlot for single-leg limit entries with no targets and compute Shadow.</li><li>Standardize slot fields set by factories, including prices, quantities, action, reserved delta, pool slot index, and signal ticks, returning DispatchOrderEnvelope results.</li></ul> | `src/V12_002.SIMA.StrategyFactories.cs` |

---

<details>
<summary>Tips and commands</summary>

#### Interacting with Sourcery

- **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request.
- **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments.
- **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an
  issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a
  review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it.
- **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull
  request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment
  `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time.
- **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in
  the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you
  want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to
  (re-)generate the summary at any time.
- **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull
  request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time.
- **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the
  pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already
  addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore.
- **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull
  request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you
  want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment
  `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review!

#### Customizing Your Experience

Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to:
- Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request
  summary, the reviewer's guide, and others.
- Change the review language.
- Add, remove or edit custom review instructions.
- Adjust other review settings.

#### Getting Help

- [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback.
- Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information.
- Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

</details>

### Antigravity Auto-Auditor (Gemini via Vertex AI)

Warning: **Gemini Auditor Critical Failure**: Failed to find any working Gemini model across regions: global, us-central1, us-east4, us-west1, europe-west1, us-east1. Please verify Vertex AI API and Billing are active.

**Recommendation**: Check GCP Console for Project 'project-263b6139-6893-4788-995'. Ensure 'Vertex AI API' is enabled and models are accessible in your region.
--
author:	sourcery-ai
association:	none
edited:	false
status:	commented
--
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- StagedTargetBuffer.At() unconditionally returns T5 for any index >= 3 and does no bounds checking, which could hide incorrect call sites; consider validating the index against Count or throwing for out-of-range values to make misuse more visible.
- IFleetAccountFilter.ShouldSkip() takes a concrete HashSet<string> for activeAccountSnapshot, which tightly couples callers and implementations to that specific collection type; consider relaxing this to IReadOnlyCollection<string> (or similar) to improve flexibility and testability.
- IFollowerPricingEngine.ComputeFollowerPrices() exposes six out parameters for targets, which can be error-prone at call sites; you might consider returning a small value object (e.g., a struct with Stop, T1ÔÇôT5) to make the API easier to consume and less order-dependent.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- StagedTargetBuffer.At() unconditionally returns T5 for any index >= 3 and does no bounds checking, which could hide incorrect call sites; consider validating the index against Count or throwing for out-of-range values to make misuse more visible.
- IFleetAccountFilter.ShouldSkip() takes a concrete HashSet<string> for activeAccountSnapshot, which tightly couples callers and implementations to that specific collection type; consider relaxing this to IReadOnlyCollection<string> (or similar) to improve flexibility and testability.
- IFollowerPricingEngine.ComputeFollowerPrices() exposes six out parameters for targets, which can be error-prone at call sites; you might consider returning a small value object (e.g., a struct with Stop, T1ÔÇôT5) to make the API easier to consume and less order-dependent.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="src/V12_002.SIMA.StrategyFactories.cs" line_range="70-76" />
<code_context>
+                Count = Math.Min(5, Count + 1);
+            }
+
+            public StagedTarget At(int index)
+            {
+                if (index == 0) return T1;
+                if (index == 1) return T2;
+                if (index == 2) return T3;
+                if (index == 3) return T4;
+                return T5;
+            }
+        }
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue (bug_risk):** At() does not guard against out-of-range indices, which can return an uninitialized or stale target.

When `index >= Count`, `At()` falls through to `T5` and may return default or stale data with no indication of misuse. Please either validate the index (e.g., throw, clamp, or return a sentinel) or clearly document and assert that callers must ensure `0 <= index < Count` (e.g., via a debug assertion).
</issue_to_address>

### Comment 2
<location path="src/V12_002.SIMA.StrategyFactories.cs" line_range="51-58" />
<code_context>
+            public StagedTarget T4;
+            public StagedTarget T5;
+
+            public void Add(in StagedTarget target)
+            {
+                if (Count == 0) T1 = target;
+                else if (Count == 1) T2 = target;
+                else if (Count == 2) T3 = target;
+                else if (Count == 3) T4 = target;
+                else if (Count == 4) T5 = target;
+                Count = Math.Min(5, Count + 1);
+            }
+
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion (bug_risk):** StagedTargetBuffer.Add silently drops additional targets after 5 without any indication.

After 5 elements, `Add` stops storing new targets but still returns successfully, which can hide dropped targets. If this hard cap is intentional, consider making it explicit (e.g., early-return when `Count >= 5`, or a comment/assert) so future maintainers donÔÇÖt assume `Add` always appends. If itÔÇÖs not intentional, this behavior will cause targets to be silently lost.

```suggestion
        private struct StagedTargetBuffer
        {
            public int Count;
            public StagedTarget T1;
            public StagedTarget T2;
            public StagedTarget T3;
            public StagedTarget T4;
            public StagedTarget T5;

            /// <summary>
            /// Adds a staged target up to the fixed capacity of 5.
            /// Targets beyond this capacity are intentionally ignored.
            /// </summary>
            /// <remarks>
            /// This buffer is a small fixed-capacity struct used to avoid allocations.
            /// If you need to handle more than 5 targets, refactor the caller to use
            /// a dynamic collection instead of this buffer.
            /// </remarks>
            public void Add(in StagedTarget target)
            {
                // Fixed capacity: explicitly ignore additional targets once full.
                if (Count >= 5)
                    return;

                if (Count == 0) T1 = target;
                else if (Count == 1) T2 = target;
                else if (Count == 2) T3 = target;
                else if (Count == 3) T4 = target;
                else if (Count == 4) T5 = target;

                Count++;
            }
```
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

***

<details>
<summary>Sourcery is free for open source - if you like our reviews please consider sharing them Ô£¿</summary>

- [Mastodon](https://mastodon.social/share?text=I%20just%20got%20an%20instant%20code%20review%20from%20%40SourceryAI%2C%20and%20it%20was%20brilliant%21%20It%27s%20free%20for%20open%20source%20and%20has%20a%20free%20trial%20for%20private%20code.%20Check%20it%20out%20https%3A//sourcery.ai)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https://sourcery.ai)
- [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://sourcery.ai)

</details>

<sub>
Help me be more useful! Please click ­ƒæì or ­ƒæÄ on each comment and I'll use the feedback to improve your reviews.
</sub>
--
author:	gemini-code-assist
association:	none
edited:	false
status:	commented
--
## Code Review

This pull request introduces several interfaces and structures to decouple strategy factories and dispatch logic, including IFleetAccountFilter, IFollowerPricingEngine, and command factories for market and limit orders. Feedback was provided regarding the StagedTargetBuffer.At method, which lacks proper bounds checking for the index parameter, potentially leading to silent logic errors. A more robust implementation with explicit exception handling for out-of-bounds access was suggested.
--
author:	kilo-code-bot
association:	none
edited:	true
status:	none
--
<!-- kilo-review -->
## Code Review Summary

**Status:** No Issues Found | **Recommendation:** Merge

<details>
<summary><b>Files Reviewed (1 files)</b></summary>

- `src/V12_002.SIMA.StrategyFactories.cs` - New Phase 3 strategy factories and dispatch decoupling contracts (interfaces: IFleetAccountFilter, IFollowerPricingEngine, IFollowerFSMBuilder; structs: DispatchOrderEnvelope, StagedTargetBuffer, MarketOrderCommandFactory, LimitOrderCommandFactory). Ô£à

</details>

---
<!-- kilo-usage -->
<sub>Reviewed by ling-2.6-1t-20260423:free ┬À 7,209,749 tokens</sub>
--
